concept.title: Map-First Preprocessing (preprocess2) in 4.x
concept.shortdesc: DITA-OT's map-first preprocessing completes map-level resolution before topic processing, simplifying key/scope handling and enabling topic-level filtering optimization.

section.title: Why map-first exists
section.p: DITA-OT provides a map-first preprocessing approach as an alternative to the default preprocess. It provides the same functionality but changes the processing model: it completes most map processing first and only begins topic processing after map-level features are resolved.

Later DITA versions added many map-level features (keys, key scopes, etc.) that make it harder to reliably process topics before map features are resolved. The map-first model formalizes "process the map first," simplifies responsibilities, and can process only the topics actually referenced after filtering.

section.title: 4.x impact: HTML5 uses map-first as of 4.2
section.p: DITA-OT notes that map-first preprocessing is enabled by default for:

ul.li: PDF and HTML Help as of 3.0
HTML5 as of 4.2 (after further testing and feedback)

section.p: Developer consequence: extension points available only in legacy preprocess pipelines might not apply, and step-specific hooks may be unavailable in preprocess2.

section.title: Calling preprocess2 in a custom transtype
section.p: The docs describe updating a custom transtype's Ant target dependencies to call preprocess2 instead of preprocess, and they show example depends chains for HTML5 variants.
Rule for teams: if you maintain production transforms used by many people, consider a "beta" transtype that is identical except it uses preprocess2 so teams can test safely.
